storyBeat: 世界上沒有故事 — 蜘蛛想去拿

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 story = 故事
🔑 night = 夜晚
🔑 sky = 天空
🔑 spider = 蜘蛛
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Long ago, there were no stories in the world.
questions.explanationZh: 很久以前,世界上沒有故事。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: People sat together at night, and no one had a tale to tell.
questions.question: What was night-time like for the people?
questions.explanationZh: 沒有人有故事說——夜晚就這樣靜悄悄的,空空的。選「很安靜很空」最準確喔!
questions.sentenceZh: 夜晚大家坐在一起,可是沒有人有故事可說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: All the stories belonged to Nyame, the Sky God.
questions.questionEn: Could people on the ground share any story?
questions.explanationZh: 故事全都是天神的——地上的人一個都拿不到。所以「能不能分享故事?」答案是 No 囉。
questions.sentenceZh: 所有的故事都屬於天神 Nyame。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A small spider named Anansi looked up at the sky.
questions.explanationZh: 一隻名叫 Anansi 的小蜘蛛抬頭看著天空。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi was tiny, but his head was full of plans.
questions.question: What kind of spider was Anansi?
questions.explanationZh: 「tiny」是很小,「head full of plans」是腦袋裝滿點子——所以 Anansi 是「小但點子多」!
questions.sentenceZh: Anansi 很小,但腦袋裡裝滿了計畫。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He wanted everyone on the ground to have a story to share.
questions.explanationZh: 他希望地上每個人都有故事可以分享。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: So he made a long thin thread that went all the way up.
questions.question: What did Anansi make to reach the sky?
questions.explanationZh: 「long thin thread」——細細長長往上的絲線,就是「高高的絲繩」啦!
questions.sentenceZh: 他織了一條細細長長的絲線,一路往上延伸。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The thread shone in the moonlight like a silver road.
questions.explanationZh: 那條線在月光下像一條銀色的路。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: How did Anansi feel as he started his climb?
questions.question: How did Anansi feel?
questions.explanationZh: 小蜘蛛自己決定要爬上天,一點都不猶豫——這就是「勇敢準備好」的感覺呀!
questions.sentenceZh: Anansi 開始往上爬的時候,心裡是什麼感覺?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi put one foot on the silver thread and began to climb...
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 把一隻腳放上銀線,開始往上爬……

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Long ago, there were no stories in the world.
questions.question: True or False: In the beginning, stories already existed everywhere.
questions.explanationZh: 「there were no stories」說明故事一開始根本不存在!答案是 False。
questions.sentenceZh: 很久以前,世界上沒有任何故事。
questions.questionEn: True or False: In the beginning, stories already existed everywhere.

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: All the stories belonged to Nyame, the Sky God.
questions.questionEn: Did the people on the ground own any stories?
questions.explanationZh: 故事全是天神的——地上的人一個都沒有。答案是 No!
questions.sentenceZh: 所有故事都屬於天神 Nyame。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A small spider named Anansi looked up at the sky.
questions.question: True or False: The main character is a large elephant.
questions.explanationZh: 阿南西是「small spider(小蜘蛛)」,不是大象!答案是 False。
questions.sentenceZh: 一隻名叫阿南西的小蜘蛛抬頭望向天空。
questions.questionEn: True or False: The main character is a large elephant.

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match the words from this lesson.
questions.explanationZh:
🔑 moonlight = 月光
🔑 silver = 銀色
🔑 climb = 爬
🔑 thin = 細
這些字讓故事開場更美!

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi was tiny, but his head was full of plans.
questions.question: What makes Anansi different from other small creatures?
questions.explanationZh: 「head full of plans」——腦袋裡裝滿計畫!這就是「clever ideas inside」的意思。
questions.sentenceZh: Anansi 很小,但腦袋裡裝滿了計畫。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The thread shone in the moonlight like a silver road.
questions.question: What does the simile compare the thread to?
questions.explanationZh: 「like a silver road」把蜘蛛絲比喻成閃亮的銀色小路!
questions.sentenceZh: 那根絲在月光下閃閃發光,像一條銀色的道路。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He wanted everyone on the ground to have a story to share.
questions.questionEn: Was Anansi thinking only about himself?
questions.explanationZh: 他希望「every one on the ground」——是為了所有人,不是只為自己。答案是 No!
questions.sentenceZh: 他希望地上每個人都有故事可以分享。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi put one foot on the silver thread and began to climb.
questions.question: Which direction is Anansi going?
questions.explanationZh: 「began to climb」沿著線往上爬——當然是「向上,去天空」找天神囉!
questions.sentenceZh: Anansi 踩上銀線,開始往上爬。

storyBeat: 天神開價 — 抓三個動物

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 sky god = 天神
🔑 buy = 買
🔑 price = 價錢
🔑 animal = 動物
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi went up the long thread and stood before Nyame.
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 順著長線爬上去,站在 Nyame 面前。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He bent his small head low to greet the Sky God.
questions.question: How did Anansi greet Nyame?
questions.explanationZh: 「bent his small head low」——低著頭,就是在鞠躬啦。答案是「低頭鞠躬」。
questions.sentenceZh: 他低下小小的頭,向天神打招呼。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi spoke in a soft, polite voice to the Sky God.
questions.questionEn: Did Anansi try to be respectful?
questions.explanationZh: 「soft, polite voice」——說話輕柔又有禮貌,當然是想表示尊重囉。答案是 Yes!
questions.sentenceZh: Anansi 用輕柔有禮的聲音跟天神說話。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I want to buy all your stories," Anansi said.
questions.explanationZh: 「我想買下你所有的故事,」Anansi 說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Nyame looked down and laughed at the small spider.
questions.question: What did Nyame do at first?
options: made fun of the tiny visitor
ran away fast behind a cloud
agreed right away with a smile
began to cry soft, quiet tears
questions.explanationZh: 天神「looked down + laughed」——俯視又笑,就是在取笑這個小小的訪客嘛!
questions.sentenceZh: Nyame 低頭看著小蜘蛛,笑了起來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "The price is high," Nyame said. "Bring me three animals."
questions.explanationZh: 「價錢很高,」Nyame 說。「給我帶三個動物來。」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The list was a swarm of hornets, a long python, and a leopard.
questions.question: What was on the list?
questions.explanationZh: 胡蜂、蟒蛇、豹——三個都是厲害的動物,所以選「三個強的動物」!
questions.sentenceZh: 清單上是一群胡蜂、一條長蟒蛇,還有一隻豹。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I can do this," Anansi said. He did not even blink.
questions.explanationZh: 「我可以辦到,」Anansi 說。他連眼睛都沒眨。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Sky God set the price, and the spider said yes right away.
questions.question: What is this scene mainly about?
options: a big fight high up in the sky
a long song the spider sang
a deal between a god and a spider
questions.explanationZh: 天神開條件,蜘蛛答應——這一幕就是在講兩個人的交易嘛。答案是「天神和蜘蛛的交易」!
questions.sentenceZh: 天神開出了價碼,蜘蛛馬上就答應了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi climbed down to the ground and looked at the list in his hand...
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 爬回地上,看著手中的清單……

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi went up the long thread and stood before Nyame.
questions.question: Where does Anansi stand at the start of this lesson?
questions.explanationZh: 「stood before Nyame」——站在 Nyame 面前!就是「in front of Nyame」。
questions.sentenceZh: Anansi 爬上長線,站在 Nyame 面前。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Nyame looked down and laughed at the small spider.
questions.questionEn: Did the Sky God treat Anansi as an equal at first?
questions.explanationZh: 笑他、俯視他——哪裡是當作平等的朋友?答案是 No,一開始天神看不起他!
questions.sentenceZh: Nyame 低頭看著小蜘蛛,笑了起來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I want to buy all your stories," Anansi said.
questions.question: What does Anansi ask the Sky God for?
options: a new home in the sky
questions.explanationZh: 「buy all your stories」= 想擁有所有故事!
questions.sentenceZh: 「我想買下你所有的故事,」阿南西說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match the words from this lesson.
questions.explanationZh:
🔑 bow = 彎腰
🔑 laugh = 笑
🔑 speak = 開口說
🔑 bring back = 拿回
這節和天神見面的關鍵字!

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "The price is high," Nyame said. "Bring me three animals."
questions.question: What is the price Nyame sets?
options: build a house in the sky
questions.explanationZh: 「Bring me three animals」——帶三個動物來,就是「catch three animals」的任務!
questions.sentenceZh: 「價錢很高,」Nyame 說。「帶三個動物來給我。」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I can do this," Anansi said. He did not even blink.
questions.questionEn: Was Anansi afraid of the difficult task?
questions.explanationZh: 「did not even blink」——眼都不眨,一點都不害怕。答案是 No,Anansi 非常有信心!
questions.sentenceZh: 「我可以辦到,」Anansi 說。他連眼睛都沒眨一下。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi climbed down to the ground and looked at the list in his hand.
questions.question: How does Anansi feel about the list?
questions.explanationZh: 沒有眨眼、自信說「我能辦到」——所以是「冷靜思考著」怎麼完成任務。
questions.sentenceZh: Anansi 爬回地上,看著手中的清單。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Sky God set the price, and the spider said yes right away.
questions.question: What does this scene show about Anansi's character?
questions.explanationZh: 面對高難度條件馬上答應——這就是「勇敢又充滿信心」的 Anansi!
questions.sentenceZh: 天神開出條件,蜘蛛馬上就答應了。

storyBeat: 抓蜂 — 用一片葉子和一個鍋

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 hornet = 大黃蜂
🔑 leaf = 葉子
🔑 roof = 屋頂
🔑 pot = 鍋
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The first animal was a group of hornets in a big tree.
questions.explanationZh: 第一個動物是住在大樹上的一群蜂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The tree was full of a loud, busy buzzing sound.
questions.question: What did the tree sound like?
questions.explanationZh: 「loud, busy buzzing」——大聲又忙碌的嗡嗡嗡,就是「吵又忙的嗡嗡聲」啦!
questions.sentenceZh: 那棵樹裡充滿了響亮、忙碌的嗡嗡聲。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The other animals walked far around the tree, not close to it.
questions.questionEn: Was the hornet tree a safe place for most animals?
questions.explanationZh: 大家都「繞遠路走」——代表那裡不安全嘛。所以「大黃蜂樹是安全的嗎?」答案是 No!
questions.sentenceZh: 其他動物從遠遠的地方繞開那棵樹,根本不敢靠近。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi cut a big leaf and held it over his head like a roof.
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 剪了一大片葉子,舉在頭上像屋頂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Now he had a small green roof on top of his head.
questions.question: Why was the leaf over his head?
options: to make him look very pretty
to keep something off his head
to make music in the soft wind
questions.explanationZh: 頭頂上舉著葉子當屋頂——當然是要「擋住東西」,不讓蜂螫到頭囉!
questions.sentenceZh: 現在他頭頂上有一片小小的綠色屋頂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He filled a pot with water and poured some on the hornet tree.
questions.explanationZh: 他裝滿一鍋水,把水倒在蜂的樹上。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hornets thought a sudden rain had come too soon.
questions.question: What did the hornets think was happening?
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 把水倒在樹上,大黃蜂「thought rain came」——以為下雨了,所以選「下雨來了」!
questions.sentenceZh: 大黃蜂以為突然下起了雨。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "The rain is coming! Quick, get in my pot!" Anansi called.
questions.explanationZh: 「下雨了!快進我的鍋!」Anansi 喊。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: What did the hornets do when they heard him?
questions.question: What did the hornets do?
questions.explanationZh: 大黃蜂怕雨又聽到「快進鍋!」的喊聲——就「衝進鍋裡」啦,結果被騙了!
questions.sentenceZh: 大黃蜂聽到 Anansi 呼喊,做了什麼?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi put a lid on the pot. The hornets had given up and gone inside...
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 把蓋子蓋上。蜂全都放棄,進到鍋裡了……

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The first animal was a group of hornets in a big tree.
questions.question: Where do the hornets live?
questions.explanationZh: 「hornets in a big tree」——大黃蜂就住在大樹裡!
questions.sentenceZh: 第一個動物是住在大樹上的一群蜂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi cut a big leaf and held it over his head like a roof.
questions.questionEn: Did Anansi use a tool to protect himself from the hornets?
questions.explanationZh: 用葉子當屋頂保護頭——這就是他的防護工具!答案是 Yes,他很聰明地用了工具!
questions.sentenceZh: Anansi 剪了一大片葉子,舉在頭上當屋頂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match the words from this lesson.
questions.explanationZh:
🔑 leaf = 葉子
🔑 pot = 鍋
🔑 cover = 蓋
🔑 rain = 雨
捉黃蜂的計謀工具!

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He filled a pot with water and poured some on the hornet tree.
questions.question: What liquid did Anansi use to trick the hornets?
questions.explanationZh: 「filled a pot with water」——阿南西用的是普通的水!
questions.sentenceZh: 他把一個罐子裝滿水,然後把水潑在黃蜂的樹上。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "The rain is coming! Quick, get in my pot!" Anansi called.
questions.question: Why do the hornets believe Anansi's warning?
questions.explanationZh: 阿南西事先灑了水——黃蜂感覺到水滴,以為真的下雨了!
questions.sentenceZh: 「雨要來了!快,進我的罐子裡!」阿南西喊道。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi put a lid on the pot. The hornets had gone inside.
questions.questionEn: Did the hornets escape from the pot?
questions.explanationZh: 蓋子蓋上,蜂全都進去了——沒有逃走。答案是 No,計謀成功!
questions.sentenceZh: Anansi 把蓋子蓋上。蜂全都進到鍋裡了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi put a lid on the pot and smiled.
questions.question: Which emoji shows how Anansi feels at this moment?
questions.explanationZh: 阿南西蓋上蓋子,微笑——牠為自己的成功感到得意!
questions.sentenceZh: 阿南西在罐子上蓋上蓋子,微笑了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hornets thought a sudden rain had come too soon.
questions.question: What made Anansi's plan so clever?
options: turning their fear of rain against them
making a very loud scary sound
bringing a large strong net along
optionsZh: 發出非常大的嚇人聲音
帶來一個大而結實的網
questions.explanationZh: 阿南西利用黃蜂怕雨這個弱點——這才是最聰明的地方!
questions.sentenceZh: 黃蜂以為突然下起雨來了。

storyBeat: 抓蛇 — 用一根樹枝和一個聰明話

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 python = 大蛇 (Python)
🔑 stick = 樹枝
🔑 friend = 朋友
🔑 tie = 綁
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The second animal was a long python in the river grass.
questions.explanationZh: 第二個動物是河邊草叢裡的一條長蛇。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His body went on and on, like a green road in the grass.
questions.question: How was the python's body?
questions.explanationZh: 「on and on, like a road」——一直延伸,像一條路,就是「很長像一條路」!
questions.sentenceZh: 他的身體一路延伸,像草叢裡的一條綠色道路。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The python often showed off his long body to the other animals.
questions.questionEn: Was the python proud of how long he was?
questions.explanationZh: 「常常炫耀身長」——這就是驕傲的表現嘛。答案是 Yes,大蛇很以自己的長度為傲。
questions.sentenceZh: 大蛇常常向其他動物炫耀自己的長身體。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi found a strong stick and brought it to the python.
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 找了一根結實的樹枝,拿到大蛇面前。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "My friend says you are not really that long," Anansi said.
questions.question: What did Anansi tell the python?
options: that he was the king of all snakes
that he could fly over the trees
that some friend doubted his length
that the river was dry this year
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 說「朋友覺得你沒那麼長」——就是有朋友「懷疑他的長度」,這句話是故意激大蛇的!
questions.sentenceZh: 「我朋友說你其實沒有那麼長,」Anansi 說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The python lifted his head. His eyes went sharp and bright.
questions.explanationZh: 大蛇抬起頭。他的眼睛變得銳利又閃亮。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He wanted to show everyone how very long he really was.
questions.question: How did the python feel after hearing Anansi?
questions.explanationZh: 「wanted to show how long」——急著要展示自己——這就是「想要炫耀」的心情!
questions.sentenceZh: 他想讓所有人都看到自己究竟有多長。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The python lay down by the stick to show his full length.
questions.explanationZh: 大蛇躺在樹枝旁,要展現完整的長度。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: What did Anansi do while the python was lying still?
questions.question: What did Anansi do next?
questions.explanationZh: 大蛇躺好了,Anansi 手上又有樹枝——當然是「把他綁在樹枝上」啦,計謀成功!
questions.sentenceZh: 大蛇乖乖躺著不動的時候,Anansi 做了什麼?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The python tried to move, but the stick held him safely. Anansi smiled...
questions.explanationZh: 大蛇想動,但樹枝把他穩穩固定住。Anansi 笑了……

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The second animal was a long python found near the river.
questions.question: Where does the python make its home?
questions.explanationZh: 故事說蟒蛇在「river grass / near the river」——所以住在水邊!
questions.sentenceZh: 第二隻動物是一條長蟒蛇,住在河邊。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The python often showed off his long body to the other animals.
questions.questionEn: Was the python humble about his body?
questions.explanationZh: 常常炫耀——這是驕傲不謙虛。答案是 No,他不謙虛!
questions.sentenceZh: 大蛇常常向其他動物炫耀自己的長身體。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match the words from this lesson.
questions.explanationZh:
🔑 proud = 傲慢
🔑 measure = 量
🔑 length = 長度
🔑 challenge = 挑戰
捉大蛇的計謀關鍵字!

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "My friend says you are not really that long," Anansi said.
questions.question: Why does Anansi say his friend doubts the python?
options: begging for a piece of food
getting the python to prove his length
optionsZh: 讓蟒蛇伸直身體來證明
questions.explanationZh: 阿南西故意說「我朋友說你不夠長」——蟒蛇就會伸直身體去證明!
questions.sentenceZh: 「我朋友說你其實沒那麼長,」阿南西說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The python lay down by the stick to show his full length.
questions.question: What does the python do next to Anansi's stick?
questions.explanationZh: 「lay down by the stick」——躺在樹枝旁邊,讓人量他的長度!答案是「lie down fully beside it」。
questions.sentenceZh: 大蛇躺在樹枝旁,要展現完整的長度。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The python tried to move, but the stick held him safely.
questions.questionEn: Was Anansi's second plan also a success?
questions.explanationZh: 大蛇被固定住動不了——計謀成功了!答案是 Yes!
questions.sentenceZh: 大蛇想動,但樹枝把他牢牢固定住了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The python felt proud, so he lay flat next to the stick.
questions.question: What feeling did Anansi use against the python?
questions.explanationZh: 大蛇最在乎自己有多長——Anansi 利用了他的「驕傲」來讓他乖乖躺好!
questions.sentenceZh: 大蛇覺得驕傲,所以乖乖躺在樹枝旁邊。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The python lay down by the stick to show his full length.
questions.question: What was Anansi's clever trick with the python?
options: use his pride to trap him
dig a hole in his path
questions.explanationZh: 和黃蜂不一樣——這次是「用驕傲來困住他」,大蛇因為炫耀而中計!
questions.sentenceZh: 大蛇躺在樹枝旁邊,想展示自己的完整長度。

storyBeat: 抓豹 — 挖一個洞,等夜晚

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 leopard = 豹 (Leopard)
🔑 dig = 挖
🔑 hole = 洞
🔑 rope = 繩子
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The third animal was a leopard with sharp, bright eyes.
questions.explanationZh: 第三個動物是一隻眼睛銳利明亮的豹。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: No animal in the forest dared to walk near his rest tree.
questions.question: What did the forest animals do near his rest tree?
questions.explanationZh: 「no animal dared walk near」——沒有動物敢靠近,大家都「離得遠遠的」!
questions.sentenceZh: 森林裡沒有動物敢走近他休息的那棵樹。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: For two days, Anansi watched which path the leopard used at night.
questions.questionEn: Did Anansi plan his trap with care?
questions.explanationZh: 「觀察了兩天」才行動——這就是仔細計畫嘛!答案是 Yes,Anansi 可認真了。
questions.sentenceZh: Anansi 花了兩天觀察豹子晚上走哪條路。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi dug a deep hole on the path the leopard liked.
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 在豹喜歡走的路上挖了一個深洞。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He covered the hole with leaves so no one could see it.
questions.question: Why did Anansi put leaves on top?
options: to hide the hole from view
questions.explanationZh: 「cover so no one sees」——蓋起來就是「讓人看不到洞」,這樣豹才會踩進去呀!
questions.sentenceZh: 他用葉子把洞蓋起來,這樣大家就看不到。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: That night the leopard walked the path with quiet, strong feet.
questions.explanationZh: 那天晚上,豹用安靜有力的腳走過那條路。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: One step on the leaves, and his big body went straight down.
questions.question: What happened to the leopard?
options: he sat down on a big rock
questions.explanationZh: 「body went straight down」——直直往下,就是「掉進洞裡」了。計謀奏效!
questions.sentenceZh: 踩上葉子的那一步,他的大身體就直直往下掉。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The leopard tried to climb out, but the walls were too high.
questions.explanationZh: 豹想爬出來,但洞壁太高了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi looked down at him and held out a long, strong rope.
questions.question: Why did Anansi bring a rope?
options: to lift the leopard up safely
to tie his own shoes tightly
to draw a line on the ground
questions.explanationZh: 豹在洞底出不來,Anansi 拿著繩子——當然是要「把豹安全拉上來」,好把他交給天神!
questions.sentenceZh: Anansi 俯身看著他,伸出一條長長結實的繩子。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I will help you up," Anansi said. The leopard looked up at the rope...
questions.explanationZh: 「我幫你上來,」Anansi 說。豹抬頭看著繩子……

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The third animal was a leopard with sharp, bright eyes.
questions.question: True or False: The third animal Anansi must catch is a tiger.
questions.explanationZh: 第三隻動物是「leopard(豹)」,不是老虎!答案是 False。
questions.sentenceZh: 第三隻動物是一隻有著明亮眼睛的豹。
questions.questionEn: True or False: The third animal Anansi must catch is a tiger.

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: For two days, Anansi watched which path the leopard used at night.
questions.questionEn: Did Anansi study the leopard before making his trap?
questions.explanationZh: 「watched for two days」——觀察了兩天,是仔細研究過的!答案是 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: Anansi 花了兩天觀察豹子晚上走哪條路。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match the words from this lesson.
questions.explanationZh:
🔑 hole = 洞
🔑 leaves = 葉子
🔑 path = 路
🔑 rope = 繩子
捉豹的計謀工具!

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He covered the hole with leaves so no one could see it.
questions.question: What was the purpose of hiding the hole with leaves?
options: luring the leopard into a trap
questions.explanationZh: 蓋住洞讓豹看不到——踩下去就落入陷阱!
questions.sentenceZh: 他用樹葉蓋住洞口,讓人看不見。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: One step on the leaves, and his big body went straight down.
questions.question: What happened the moment the leopard stepped on the leaves?
questions.explanationZh: 「his big body went straight down」——豹直接掉進洞裡了!
questions.sentenceZh: 踩上樹葉的那一刻,牠的大身體直直往下掉。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The leopard tried to climb out, but the walls were too high.
questions.questionEn: Could the leopard get out of the hole on his own?
questions.explanationZh: 「walls too high」——洞壁太高爬不上去。答案是 No,他自己出不來!
questions.sentenceZh: 豹想爬出來,但洞壁太高了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi watched for two days before he set the trap.
questions.question: What quality does Anansi show here?
questions.explanationZh: 兩天都在靜靜觀察——這就是「耐心」的表現!不急不躁,等待最好的時機。
questions.sentenceZh: Anansi 觀察了兩天才設下陷阱。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi looked down at him and held out a long, strong rope.
questions.question: What is Anansi's plan for the leopard now?
options: let him stay in the pit
pull him up with the rope
questions.explanationZh: 拿繩子伸下去——當然是要「用繩子拉他上來」,然後交給天神!
questions.sentenceZh: Anansi 俯身看著他,伸出一條長長結實的繩子。

storyBeat: 蜘蛛全帶回天神 — 天神給故事

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 bring = 帶
🔑 head = 頭
🔑 box = 盒子
🔑 world = 世界
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi climbed up the thread again, and this time he was not alone.
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 又爬上那條線,這次他不是一個人。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Behind him came the hornets, the long python, and the spotted leopard.
questions.question: Who came up with Anansi?
options: two old men from the town
questions.explanationZh: 大黃蜂、蟒蛇、豹——就是他辛苦抓來的「三個動物」都跟著一起上去了!
questions.sentenceZh: 跟在他身後的,是一群大黃蜂、一條長蟒蛇,還有一隻花豹。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Nyame slowly stood up from his big chair to take a closer look.
questions.questionEn: Was the Sky God surprised by what he saw?
questions.explanationZh: 天神「站起來走近看」——平常坐著就好,特意站起來,說明他很驚訝!答案是 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: Nyame 慢慢從大椅子上站起來,走近仔細看。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Sky God looked at the hornets, the python, and the leopard.
questions.explanationZh: 天神看著蜂、大蛇,還有豹。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He could not see any rope on his back or any cut on his skin.
questions.question: What did Nyame notice about the catch?
questions.explanationZh: 「no rope + no cut」——沒有傷痕,代表「沒有動物受傷」。Anansi 用計謀,不用蠻力!
questions.sentenceZh: 他看不到任何繩子或傷口。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "You are small, but your head is big," Nyame said with a smile.
questions.explanationZh: 「你很小,但腦袋很大,」Nyame 笑著說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He meant Anansi had used his clever ideas, not his strong arms.
questions.question: What did Nyame mean by "your head is big"?
questions.explanationZh: 「head is big」是比喻腦袋聰明——不是真的頭大,是「Anansi 很聰明」的意思喔!
questions.sentenceZh: 天神的意思是,Anansi 靠的是聰明的點子,不是強壯的手臂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He gave Anansi a wooden box full of all the stories of the world.
questions.explanationZh: 他給了 Anansi 一個木盒,裡面裝著全世界所有的故事。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: How did Anansi feel when he held the box?
questions.question: How did Anansi feel?
questions.explanationZh: 一隻小蜘蛛拿到了全世界所有的故事——這份成就感,當然是「驕傲又開心」啦!
questions.sentenceZh: Anansi 抱著盒子的時候,心裡是什麼感覺?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi held the box close to his chest and looked down at the world below...
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 把盒子抱在胸前,看著下面的世界……

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi climbed up the thread again, and this time he was not alone.
questions.question: What was different about this second climb up?
questions.explanationZh: 「not alone」——這次不是一個人了!答案是「he had company this time」。
questions.sentenceZh: Anansi 再次爬上那條線,這次他不是一個人。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Nyame slowly stood up from his big chair to take a closer look.
questions.questionEn: Did Nyame expect Anansi to succeed?
questions.explanationZh: 他「慢慢站起來仔細看」——驚訝才需要這樣!他沒有預料到成功。答案是 No!
questions.sentenceZh: Nyame 慢慢從大椅子上站起來,走近仔細看。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match the words from this lesson.
questions.explanationZh:
🔑 bring back = 帶回
🔑 nod = 點頭
🔑 surprised = 驚訝
🔑 smile = 微笑
天神驚喜的關鍵字!

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "You are small, but your head is big," Nyame said with a smile.
questions.question: What does Nyame mean by this compliment?
questions.explanationZh: 「your head is big」不是說頭大,而是誇阿南西腦袋聰明!
questions.sentenceZh: 「你雖然小,但你的腦袋很大,」尼亞梅微笑著說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He gave Anansi a wooden box full of all the stories of the world.
questions.question: What was inside the wooden box?
questions.explanationZh: 「full of all the stories of the world」——盒子裡裝的是「世界所有的故事」!
questions.sentenceZh: 他給了 Anansi 一個木盒,裡面裝著全世界所有的故事。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He could not see any rope on his back or any cut on his skin.
questions.questionEn: Did Anansi hurt any of the three animals to catch them?
questions.explanationZh: 「no rope + no cut」——沒有傷痕、沒有繩子勒痕。答案是 No,沒有用蠻力傷害他們!
questions.sentenceZh: 他看不到任何繩子或傷口。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Sky God gave Anansi a wooden box full of all the stories.
questions.question: What did the deal end with?
questions.explanationZh: 「wooden box full of stories」——交易的結果是「a box of stories」!
questions.sentenceZh: 天神給了 Anansi 一個裝滿故事的木盒。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Sky God looked at the hornets, the python, and the leopard.
questions.question: What is this whole scene showing?
options: Nyame tricked Anansi in the end
a small spider kept every promise
optionsZh: 小蜘蛛兌現了所有承諾
故事就這樣永遠消失了
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 帶回了三個動物,完成了承諾——「a small spider kept every promise」就是這幕的核心!
questions.sentenceZh: 天神看著蜂、大蛇和豹。

storyBeat: 蜘蛛把故事散到全世界

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 carry = 抱
🔑 open = 打開
🔑 lid = 蓋子
🔑 fly = 飛
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi carried the heavy box back down to the ground.
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 把重重的盒子帶回地上。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Every step down the thread was slow and careful.
questions.question: How did Anansi move on the way down?
questions.explanationZh: 「slow and careful」——慢慢地、小心地——就是「慢慢小心」啦,盒子那麼珍貴當然要謹慎!
questions.sentenceZh: 往下的每一步都走得又慢又小心。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: In the village, the people came out and made a circle around him.
questions.questionEn: Were the people curious about what was in the box?
questions.explanationZh: 大家特地「圍成一圈」——當然是很好奇盒子裡有什麼!答案是 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: 在村子裡,人們走出來,圍成一個圈看著他。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He opened the lid in the middle of the village.
questions.explanationZh: 他在村子中央把蓋子打開。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The stories flew out like birds and went in every direction.
questions.question: What did the stories look like?
questions.explanationZh: 「flew out like birds」——就是像鳥一樣「飛出來」!多美的畫面呀。
questions.sentenceZh: 故事像鳥一樣飛出來,往四面八方飛去。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: They went to every corner of the earth, near and far.
questions.explanationZh: 它們飛到地球的每個角落,近的遠的都有。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Now every home, even small ones, had a story by the fire at night.
questions.question: What changed for the people at night?
options: every home had a story to share
they all moved away to a new town
the fire went out in every home
questions.explanationZh: 「every home had a story」——每家都有故事了,所以「每家都有故事可分享」!
questions.sentenceZh: 現在每個家,就算是小小的家,夜晚火堆旁也有故事了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: And that is why, even today, there is a tale in every home.
questions.explanationZh: 所以,直到今天,每個家裡都有故事。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A small spider with a big head changed the night for everyone.
questions.question: What is the main lesson of this story?
options: only the strong can ever win the day
you should never trust a small spider
clever thinking can beat brute strength
stories belong only to great kings
optionsZh: 故事只屬於偉大的國王
questions.explanationZh: 整個故事就是 Anansi 用聰明腦袋一步步贏過強大的動物——這就說明「聰明可以勝過蠻力」呀!
questions.sentenceZh: 一隻小小的大頭蜘蛛,改變了所有人的夜晚。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Tonight your story is also one of Anansi's. What is the first story you remember?
questions.explanationZh: 今晚你聽的故事,也是 Anansi 帶回來的。你記得的第一個故事是什麼?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi carried the heavy box back down to the ground.
questions.question: What does Anansi bring back from the sky?
questions.explanationZh: 「carried the heavy box back down」——阿南西帶著沉重的木箱回到地面!
questions.sentenceZh: 阿南西把那個沉重的箱子帶回地面上。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: In the village, the people came out and made a circle around him.
questions.questionEn: Were the people in the village interested in what Anansi had?
questions.explanationZh: 大家出來圍成一圈——當然很有興趣!答案是 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: 在村子裡,人們走出來圍成一個圈看著他。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match the words from this lesson.
questions.explanationZh:
🔑 lid = 蓋子
🔑 corner = 角落
🔑 fly = 飛
🔑 every home = 每個家
故事結局的關鍵字!

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He opened the lid in the middle of the village.
questions.question: Where does Anansi open the box?
questions.explanationZh: 「in the middle of the village」——在村子中央!他要讓所有人都聽到故事。
questions.sentenceZh: 他在村子中央把蓋子打開。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The stories flew out like birds and went in every direction.
questions.question: What happens when the box is opened?
options: one story falls to the floor
birds fly into the box quickly
questions.explanationZh: 「flew out like birds + every direction」——故事立刻散到四面八方!就是「spread everywhere at once」。
questions.sentenceZh: 故事像鳥一樣飛出來,往四面八方飛去。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Now every home, even small ones, had a story by the fire at night.
questions.questionEn: Did stories reach only the big, important homes?
questions.explanationZh: 「even small ones」——連小小的家也有了!不只大屋子。答案是 No!
questions.sentenceZh: 現在每個家,就算是小小的家,夜晚火堆旁也有故事了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: And that is why, even today, there is a tale in every home.
questions.question: What did Anansi give to the world?
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 帶回了故事,讓全世界每個家都有故事了!答案是「stories for everyone」!
questions.sentenceZh: 所以直到今天,每個家裡都有故事。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A small spider with a big head changed the night for everyone.
questions.question: Why is Anansi a good hero for this story?
options: he was born with great power
optionsZh: 用智慧勝過體型和力量
questions.explanationZh: 阿南西又小又弱,卻靠智慧贏得一切——這才是他的偉大之處!
questions.sentenceZh: 一隻有著大腦袋的小蜘蛛改變了所有人的夜晚。
